comment_text:
The Deconstruction of a Masterpiece

Netflix, with its team of writers—or perhaps under the absolute command of a showrunner who simultaneously serves as creator, writer, and executive producer—has managed to dismantle a literary masterpiece that once had every chance of becoming one of the most promising adaptations in the cinematic world.

The original story already embraced diversity, emotional depth, and themes capable of inspiring genuine social reflection. Yet they allowed a professional devoid of any sense of collectivity or respect for the audience—particularly for the readers of the saga—to impose her personal impulses and desires upon the narrative. The result was a complete distortion of the literary essence and of the internal logic of the universe it belongs to.

This showrunner turned The Witcher into something unpalatable, exhausting, and intellectually offensive to anyone familiar with the source material. It is regrettable that Netflix productions persist in reshaping the audience’s perception according to their own agendas and preferences, forgetting that the true power of storytelling lies in respecting both the work itself and the audience that elevated it.

If this trajectory continues, it is only a matter of time before millions of subscribers abandon the platform. All that remains is to hope that another studio—one capable of recognizing the limits and intelligence of its viewers—acquires the rights and delivers a reboot worthy of one of the most profound and inspiring tales in modern fantasy.

comment_text:
The screenplay or whatever you call it, in All 3 seasons, was amazing, regardless to if it's the source material or not.Lets be honest, we loved the series and the stories. But the "Henry Cavill thingy" is the only thing that will keep the fourth season from having the same success.
Didn't he say during an interview with Horowitz he asked clarence from the series so he can play Superman? And that was the reason he left? He did. If no contact was bounding him to lie, maybe he was honest. 
He wanted to be Superman again and leaving "The Witcher" was necessary to make time for Superman... 
He SAID it himself being so confident he got the Superman role again.
Guess when he got the news he won't be playing Superman after all, it was too late to come back to "The Witcher". Maybe he never tried to come back. Maybe the whole Superman thing was an excuse to have more time for personal life? There are a lot of contradictions and a lot more speculations.
Either way, the problem remains.
Too many fans, including me, got used to him as the witcher. But I'm going to watch because I like what they did throughout 3 seasons. Shout-out to Netflix for the creativity! Lore or not - still was an amazing series to watch.
Escapism at its best. 
It's hard to keep up 6 full days of shooting when you're in love and want to pursue a romantic relationship. I think that's what Henry did. 
Maybe his priorities has changed once he needed time for his personal life, which he couldn't have if he continued playing in The Witcher. 
But we all still wish it was him...
I'm just gonna try to enjoy the story.
Bet seeing Liam say "hmmm f*ck" will be somewhat strange 😅 but I'm game. 
Bring it on. Hope the ending is good. Sort of "and they all lived together happily ever after" sort of ending. ❤

video_title: The Witcher: Season 4 | Official Teaser | Netflix
comment_text: What the hell happened to the Witcher? When season 1 came out, I was fully invested and I've never played the games or read the books. Season 2 I thought was good too, but when season 3 came out I definitely noticed a drop in quality and started getting bored watching it. Now we have this teaser for season 4 with Liam Hemsworth replacing Henry Cavel as Geralt? I'm sorry, but I can't bring myself to give the new season a chance knowing Cavel's been replaced. I have nothing against Liam; he's a great actor and person. It's just that Cavel did such a great job portraying him, and I think him being a massive fan of the games helped him get immersed in the role and have a good time with it. Yet another video game adaptation with a strong start is now degrading into a pile of crap. Geralt ripping the Wraith's heart out is symbolism for how the producers ripped the heart of the series out. I hope to god they reveal some kind of twist that this is an alternate universe and Cavel's Geralt shows up. He's not Superman anymore, so he has time to come back to the role he was destined for.
